meta.source_document: Court Reserve Calendar — Business Decisions & Policies
meta.notes: Machine-readable expression of all business decisions governing automated recommendations and manual management of the court reserve calendar. Edit this file (not the code) when policies change.

fixed_events.note: Recurring weekly events that are always on the schedule. Used by the recommender for saturation calculations and to avoid recommending conflicting events. Courts and max_participants are informational.

utilization.note: Target = target_pct × (courts × window_hours). Recommender fills to target where feasible under hard constraints.

weekday.note: Extended to 20:00 on 2026-04-07 to allow 6 PM event starts.

hard_constraints.precedence_order_note: Constraints are enforced in the order listed below (1 = highest priority).

1_no_same_court_overlap.description: No recommendation may occupy a time window on a court that overlaps any existing event on that court on the target day.

2_one_court_per_event.description: Each recommended event must be assigned to exactly one court.

3_max_occurrences_per_event_per_day.description: No eventId may appear more than twice on the same day across all events.

4_required_level_coverage.description: Recommendations must collectively include all five levels when possible. Levels already well-covered by existing events are skipped.
4_required_level_coverage.saturation_threshold_note: If a level already has this many sessions on the target day (across all events, not just approved ones), it is considered covered and will not be forced in Pass 1. Set to 0 to always force all levels.

5_utilization_optimization.description: Generate enough non-conflicting events to reach the utilization target if feasible under constraints 1–4.

event_duration_hours.note: All Open Play sessions must be exactly 2 hours. This is a hard rule — do not recommend 1-hour open play slots.

preferred_block_duration_hours.note: All blocks are 2 hours. 1-hour fallback removed.

spread_throughout_day.description: Distribute recommendations evenly across morning, midday, afternoon, and evening rather than front-loading the earliest slots.

recommendation_rules.target_date_note: When no date is passed to run.py, defaults to 14 days from today. Override by passing a date argument: python run.py M/D/YYYY

url_notes.EditEventOccurrence: Returns 404 — does not exist as a standalone page. Use UpdateReservation instead.
url_notes.occurrence_id: The 'Id' field returned by the schedule report API is the reservationId used in UpdateReservation. Same number, different name.
url_notes.EditOnlyCurrentOccurrence: Checkbox on UpdateReservation form — must be checked to edit only this date, not the full recurring series.

results_discord_post.description: After every booking session, results (success/failure per event) are posted back to Discord immediately.

retry.description: If any bookings fail, Discord is prompted with retry instructions. User can reply 'retry', 'retry 1,2', or 'skip'. Capped at 3 total attempts.

reply_commands.retry 1,2: Retry specific failed events by number

verification.description: After booking (and after each retry), the live schedule is re-fetched and each booked event is verified.
verification.match_strategy: event_id + start_time — more reliable than court label string matching

statuses.confirmed: Event found at correct time on correct court
statuses.wrong_court: Event found at correct time but on different court — auto-fix triggered
statuses.no_court: Event found at correct time with no court assigned — auto-fix triggered
statuses.not_found: Event not on schedule at all — re-add triggered

auto_remediation.method: fix_event_court() — navigates directly to UpdateReservation using occurrence_id if known, falls back to grid search, falls back to re-add
auto_remediation.occurrence_id_source: Preferred: Id field from live schedule API. Fallback: captured from occurrences grid after initial booking.

cli.fix_command: python run.py fix <date> (--event-id ID | --name 'fragment') [--start 'H:MM AM'] --court N [--dry-run]
cli.fix_command_description: Manually update the court assignment for a specific event occurrence. Fetches live schedule to find the occurrence ID, then edits via UpdateReservation form.

fallback_behavior.when_utilization_unreachable: Stop at maximal feasible utilization under hard constraints. Report remaining gap explicitly in court-hours and percent points.
fallback_behavior.relaxation_order: TBD — requires explicit business approval before implementation. Suggested: relax constraint 5 first (utilization), then 4 (level coverage), never relax 1–3.

traceability.note: History is fetched weekly via fetch_history.py and stored locally. Scheduler run logs go to ~/Library/Logs/court_reserve/.

governance.eventid_whitelist_change_policy: Approved event IDs may only be added or removed with explicit business approval. Update approved_events in this file; do not hardcode IDs in scheduler logic.
governance.operating_window_change_policy: Windows are configurable by day-of-week in this file.
governance.fixed_events_change_policy: Fixed events may only be added or removed with explicit business approval. Update fixed_events.events in this file.
governance.version_control: This file should be tracked in version control. Git history serves as the audit log for policy changes.
